mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0%, labor market slack suppresses corporate profit margins and consumer spending, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >65% of instances where UNRATE > 9.0%, or signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index signals strengthening supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum fails to predict positive 5-day returns during late-cycle expansions, with a negative Rank IC or return frequency below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 220.0, rising inflation expectations trigger anticipated monetary tightening and higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of observations where CPIAUCSL > 220.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding labor demand and rising household income, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.03, or the signal reverses direction in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP expansion and monthly retail sales growth confirm broad-based demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 5-day returns in >55% of triggered cases, or the interaction coefficient is statistically insignificant in predictive models.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below 90.0, contracting manufacturing output signals economic deceleration, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where INDPRO < 90.0, or the threshold signal loses predictive accuracy (Rank IC < 0.03) in walk-forward tests.
